source_pr.title: txn: refactor ts acquisition within build and execute phases
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #35377

Problem Summary:

Although we have transaction context provider now, we still update and get `forUpdateTS` from `TransactionContext`. For example, `updateForUpdateTSIfNeeded` will update `forUpdateTS` in `TransactionContext` in some cases, and we will get `forUpdateTS` from `TransactionContext` sometimes but not from transaction context provider.

It also fixes the bug in the AdviseOptimizeWithPlan within Pessimistic RR where old implementation can omit some cases. 

### What is changed and how it works?

In build and execute phases,  we use context provider to get for update ts if needed. `updateForUpdateTSIfNeeded` is removed and the update of  `forUpdateTS` in `TransactionContext` is only performed by the relevant provider.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test

symptoms: //tk.MustExec("select * from t where id in (1, 2, 3) order by id for update") (sessiontxn/isolation/main_test.go)
failure_signature: //tk.MustExec("select * from t where id in (1, 2, 3) order by id for update") (sessiontxn/isolation/main_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: //tk.MustExec("select * from t where id in (1, 2, 3) order by id for update") (sessiontxn/isolation/main_test.go)

files: executor/adapter.go, executor/batch_point_get.go, executor/builder.go, executor/executor.go, executor/point_get.go, executor/trace_test.go, sessiontxn/failpoint.go, sessiontxn/isolation/main_test.go, sessiontxn/isolation/readcommitted.go, sessiontxn/isolation/readcommitted_test.go, sessiontxn/isolation/repeatable_read.go, sessiontxn/isolation/repeatable_read_test.go, sessiontxn/isolation/repeatable_reat_test.go, sessiontxn/legacy/provider.go, sessiontxn/txn_context_test.go
